properties.name: client transport

properties.description: Task 1 — Reactive auth pivot: remove old expiry-metadata logic and add 401/403 halt path. Remove .usai_key_meta.json file and all management logic from key_manager.py (item 1). Remove 7-day expiry check in KeyManager.__init__ (item 2). Remove issued_at tracking and 4-hour buffer logic (item 3). Remove warning-at-less-than-24-hours logic (item 4). Add 401/403 halt path in worker_pool.py: halt the pool within 1 second on auth failure, preserve checkpoint state, raise clear error (item 8). Remove .usai_key_meta.json from .gitignore since file no longer exists. Sources: ADR-002, FR-010, FR-011, FR-022.

Task 2 — CredentialProvider protocol and implementations. Replace current KeyManager class in key_manager.py with CredentialProvider protocol plus three concrete implementations (item 5): DotEnvProvider (default), EnvVarProvider (CI/containers), AzureKeyVaultProvider (optional via .[azure]). DotEnvProvider must implement layered resolution: project-local .env → user-level .env → OS environment variable (item 6). User-level path per OS: $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/usai-harness/.env (default ~/.config/usai-harness/.env) on Linux/macOS, %APPDATA%\usai-harness\.env on Windows. Do NOT add platformdirs dependency — compute paths inline. AzureKeyVaultProvider implementation uses azure-identity and azure-keyvault-secrets (item 7). Sources: ADR-003, ADR-008, FR-006, FR-007, FR-009, FR-009a. Depends on Task 1.

Task 3 — Setup CLI subcommands and live model catalog merge. Implement usai-harness init (item 9): interactive setup prompting for provider name, base URL, and key via getpass.getpass; write to user-level .env; query /api/v1/models; write model catalog to user-level config; run test completion; idempotent. Implement usai-harness add-provider NAME (item 10): same pattern as init but scoped to one new provider. Implement usai-harness discover-models [PROVIDER] (item 11): query /api/v1/models for one or all providers; rewrite user-level model catalog; do not touch credentials; do not modify repo-level configs/models.yaml. Implement usai-harness verify (item 12): end-to-end health check for all providers with per-provider pass/fail. Implement usai-harness ping (item 13): minimal single-call check against default provider for scripted pre-flight. Implement usai-harness audit (item 14): gitignore coverage check, tracked-secret scan, pip-audit invocation, optional --fix-gitignore. Add live model catalog merge logic in config.py (item 15): user-level catalog is authoritative; repo-level configs/models.yaml provides presentation overrides only (display name, temp ranges, cost rates); entries in live catalog not present in overrides fall back to model ID as display name. Sources: ADR-009, FR-035, FR-036, FR-037, FR-038, FR-039, FR-040, FR-041, ADR-002 (ping), ADR-007 (audit). Depends on Task 2.

Task 4 — Security defaults. Secret redaction in logger.py and transport.py error paths (item 16): regex scrubs Bearer ... and configured key patterns from all output before writing to disk or stderr. getpass.getpass() for interactive key capture (item 17): replace any input() calls in CLI subcommands that accept API keys. TLS-verify-false warning (item 18): when verify=False is configured, emit WARNING to stderr on every call, intentionally noisy. Model echo check (item 19): log both model_requested and model_returned; flag mismatches in the post-run report. Content-logging opt-in flag (item 20): log_content=True parameter on batch(); when set, prompt and response fields are added to call log entries; default is off. Metadata-only cost ledger dataclass (item 21): ensure cost.py ledger dataclass has no content field — no field for prompt, response, content, or similar; structural guarantee, not convention. Sources: ADR-004, ADR-007, FR-028, FR-029, FR-031, FR-041, SEC-001, SEC-003.

Task 5 — Corrections, minor items, and integration test rewrite. Replace placeholder model IDs in configs/models.yaml (item 22) — current file has placeholders like llama-4-maverick; use verified IDs from /Users/brock/Documents/GitHub/usai-api-tester/config.yaml (examples: meta-llama/Llama-4-Maverick-17B-128E-Instruct, claude-sonnet-4-5-20241022, gemini-2.5-pro). Verify USAi endpoint path prefix (item 23) — usai-api-tester uses /api/v1/chat/completions and /api/v1/models; harness transport may assume /v1/...; confirm and correct if needed. Add .[azure] optional extra to pyproject.toml with azure-identity>=1.15 and azure-keyvault-secrets>=4.7 (item 24). Add requirements.lock with hashes via pip-compile --generate-hashes for reproducible installs in security-sensitive environments (item 25). Register all new subcommands in pyproject.toml CLI entry point, not just the original set (item 26). Update .env.example to reflect the new user-level storage model — file remains useful as project-local override template (item 27). Rewrite tests/integration/test_live_usai.py against reactive-auth behavior per TEVV Plan Section 4.2; the current test was written against the old 7-day-expiry design. Sources: ADR-001, ADR-003, ADR-005, ADR-009, IR-002, FR-016, FR-040, SEC-006. Depends on Tasks 1-4.
**Location:** `/Users/brock/Documents/GitHub/usai-harness/`
